events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes notching/boring beyond IRC limits on a structural framing member (joist). The notch depth vs. joist depth is measurable in a photo, and it's beyond IRC limits. This is a clear non-compliance issue.

IRC R502.8.1 specifies that notches in solid lumber joists shall not exceed 1/6 of the depth of the joist, and notches on the tension edge shall not exceed 1/6 of the depth. Bored holes shall not be larger in diameter than 1/3 the depth of the joist, and shall not be closer than 2 inches to the top or bottom edge.

Since the condition is described as "notching/boring beyond IRC limits," this is a fail. The work is rough-complete. The component is installed-defective and non-compliant.

Let me construct the JSON response.

A notch exceeding these limits is non-compliant and structurally defective at rough-complete stage.","references":["IRC R502","IRC R602"]}
